bead.title: workers: autonomous tracker git sync (fetch/merge/push) at safe boundaries so federated nodes converge on queue state
bead.description:
# PROBLEM
Federated nodes share bead queues through git, but nothing in the server-managed worker path ever runs fetch/merge/push — queues silently diverge. Observed live 2026-07-05/06:
- eitri's ddx checkout opened the session at main ahead 365 / behind 38 vs origin (weeks of worker closes never pushed; the retired /tmp factory-supervisor stopgap used to do per-cycle pull+push, server-managed workers do not).
- sindri's ddx checkout (queried via its server API /api/beads/status) reports total=2315 vs eitri's 2339 — it is missing 24 beads created on eitri including the P0 fixes ddx-57faa17c and ddx-a6e99ad3, and it holds 1 closed bead (closed=2206 vs 2205) never pushed to origin.
Latent failure: FEAT-026 partitions queue work by project owner, but ownership relies on both nodes seeing the same tracker. A worker started against a stale checkout re-executes already-closed beads or conflicts with landed work. FEAT-026's frame explicitly lists 'bead queues must be reconciled by hand' as the gap this bead closes.

# ROOT CAUSE
No component owns tracker git sync during autonomous drains. cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go / the execute-bead loop land results to LOCAL main only; nothing fetches origin or pushes after close. The old stopgap supervisor script (per-cycle clean+pull/merge+push, see ddx-171dc478 stopgap) was retired when server-managed workers replaced it, dropping the sync side-effect.

# DIRECTIVE (operator, 2026-07-06)
Force a git sync BEFORE and AFTER claiming beads. The claim boundary — not just close/idle — is the sync point:
1. PRE-CLAIM: git fetch origin + merge origin/main --no-ff before candidate selection, so the worker picks from the freshest queue and never claims a bead another node already closed or claimed.
2. POST-CLAIM: commit + push the claim immediately, publishing it so other nodes' pre-claim syncs see the bead as taken. The cross-node double-execution window shrinks to the interval between one node's post-claim push and the other's pre-claim fetch.
3. POST-CLOSE/LAND: push the close and landed result (as before).
Idle-tick sync remains as a low-frequency backstop (default ~5min) for nodes that are not claiming.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement the three sync points above in the worker loop. Merge with --no-ff only (never squash/rebase; union-merge JSONL conflicts per existing tracker-merge conventions). Pre-claim sync failure (unreachable origin) degrades to claim-from-local with a warning event — availability over freshness, matching the worker-autonomy posture of ADR-022. Push failures after claim retry with backoff, then emit loop.operator_attention rather than blocking the drain. Respect LandCoordinator (skip merge while a land is in flight). Gate behind --tracker-sync (default ON for --watch), mirroring the --no-X discoverable-opt-out convention enforced by cli/cmd/flag_design_test.go.

# NON-SCOPE
- Hub-forwarded owner-targeted writes (FEAT-026 story 14 beads ddx-3f3e1cbe, ddx-20e7ebf0, ddx-0793ac75).
- Real-time distributed claim arbitration beyond the git claim protocol above (ownership partitioning remains the primary collision guard).
- Fixing sindri's currently-stale checkout (operational one-time reconcile; this bead prevents recurrence).

Related: FEAT-026, ADR-007-federation-topology, ddx-171dc478 (closed; safe-boundary precedent).

No deps.
bead.acceptance:
1. New test TestExecuteLoop_PreClaimSyncFetchesBeforeCandidateSelection proves: with a local bare fixture remote where origin/main closed bead X after the worker's last sync, the worker fetch+merges pre-claim and does NOT claim X (claims the next ready bead instead).
2. New test TestExecuteLoop_PostClaimPushPublishesClaim proves: immediately after claim, the claim commit is pushed; a second store cloned from origin sees the bead as in_progress before implementation begins.
3. New test TestExecuteLoop_PostCloseSyncPushesClose proves: after a landed close, origin's beads.jsonl contains the close.
4. New test TestExecuteLoop_PreClaimSyncDegradesWhenOriginUnreachable proves: with origin unreachable, the worker warns and proceeds claiming from local state (no stall), and TestExecuteLoop_TrackerSyncFailureEmitsOperatorAttention proves persistent post-claim push failure emits loop.operator_attention without blocking the drain.
5. Flag audit: --tracker-sync default-on for --watch with --no-tracker-sync opt-out passes cli/cmd/flag_design_test.go conventions.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run 'TrackerSync|PreClaimSync|PostClaimPush' -count=1 green
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes
